human_verification.notes: Human verification recorded for issue #1367 validated spec-pack evidence, source baseline, functionality coverage, and boundary handling.

deployment_readiness.runtime_note: Package is deployable as a specification-based source for materialization. It does not claim a retained implementation.

materialization_requirements: tenant task and boundary metadata for cemetery administration requests
jurisdiction, cemetery type, facility policy, disclosure obligations, retention schedule, access policy, and update permission matrix

systems of record and retrieval hooks for cemetery management, interment ledger, lot-right register, plot maps, GIS, work orders, memorial records, document repository, family communication log, and audit log
authority owners for legal right of disposition, lot ownership, disinterment, veteran eligibility, public-health exceptions, financial approvals, cemetery-manager signoff, and records governance
regulated-lane caveats that prevent official vital-record, legal, licensed, safety-critical, benefit, and financial decisions
refusal_or_orchestrator_return_condition: required tenant facts, records, or authoritative context are missing and would change the administrative outcome
records conflict across permit, body receipt, lot book, interment ledger, plot map, GIS, work order, memorial, or family communication sources
request requires legal, licensed, public-health, safety-critical, official-record, veteran-eligibility, financial-approval, or cemetery-authority decision making
retention, privacy, access, disclosure, or public-record rules conflict with the requested use
the request would treat cemetery administration as generic file work and omit cemetery-specific evidence
notes: Structured readiness evidence supports the validated claim required by issue #1367.
Runtime strategy remains specification-based until a separate retained implementation is committed and promoted.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and this readiness evidence file.

No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains because jurisdiction, cemetery type, authority, access, retention, public-health, and system-specific differences are converted into required tenant facts and refusal triggers.
